title: The diffusion curve is shorter now
subtitle: How long the PC took to show up in aggregate productivity statistics vs how fast AI is showing up in measured task coverage.
caption: Sources: Paul David (1990), 'The Dynamo and the Computer' and Brynjolfsson & Hitt (2003), 'Computing Productivity: Firm-Level Evidence' for the 5–15 year PC productivity-diffusion lag (the Solow productivity paradox). Anthropic Economic Index reports, February 2025 (V1) and March 2026, for task coverage of O*NET jobs moving from 36% to 49% in ten months.

left.label: PC era
left.description: before IT productivity showed up in aggregate statistics at all. The Solow productivity paradox: investment in computing hardware exploded through the 1980s, but the productivity gains took more than a decade to register.

right.label: AI era
right.description: for the Anthropic Economic Index to move from 36% to 49% task coverage of O*NET jobs. Thirteen points in under a year on the measurement that matters most for labor displacement.
